findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: Authentication Security Standards Not Aligned to Goal
Critical Business Process Missing: Payment and Checkout
Critical Business Process Missing: Order Management
Payment Processing Missing
Order Management System Missing
Payment Gateway Integration Completely Missing
Currency persistence mechanism requires explicit clarification
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: DeFOSPAM finding P-001 identifies that REQ001 lacks clarity on currency preference persistence. Examples created assume browser cookie-based persistence with 1-year expiration for non-authenticated users and database persistence for logged-in users. Without explicit specification, implementation may vary.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: Inventory validation at add-to-cart requires explicit enforcement point
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: DeFOSPAM finding P-003 identifies critical gap: REQ004 lacks inventory validation rules. Examples show validation must occur at add-to-cart time with clear error messaging. Without specification, system may allow overselling or fail to validate stock before checkout.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: Product image failure handling requires graceful degradation specification
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: DeFOSPAM finding P-004 identifies critical gap: REQ008 requires product images but lacks failure handling. Examples demonstrate product remains purchasable with placeholder image when load fails. Without specification, image failures may render products unavailable.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: Inventory Validation Logic Critically Missing from Shopping Cart Specification
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: Shopping Cart feature lacks explicit specification for inventory validation when adding products. Two critical findings (D-003 in openrequirements-results: confidence 9, severity critical) identify mi

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: Inventory Management Missing
Inventory Management System Missing
Security implementation standards require explicit technical specification
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: DeFOSPAM finding A-001 and S-001 identify that REQ002 uses vague term 'securely' without defining implementation. Examples assume: TLS 1.3 encryption, bcrypt password hashing, 60-minute session timeout, 3-attempt account lockout. Without explicit standards, security implementation will be inconsistent.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: DeFOSPAM scenarios contain excessive UI procedural instructions
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: All 20 DeFOSPAM scenarios embed UI interaction details such as 'clicks', 'enters', 'submits form', 'on the homepage', 'in the footer' that prescribe HOW users accomplish tasks rather than specifying W

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: Scenarios reflect UI structure rather than domain model
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: DeFOSPAM scenarios frequently reference UI location and structure (homepage, footer, dropdown, banner, search bar) instead of domain concepts. This suggests requirements were written from a prototype

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: Critical External Dependencies Create Flakiness Risk in Search and Cart Features
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: Scenarios involving search (autocomplete suggestions), shopping cart (inventory validation), and product discovery depend on external services: search engine indexing, inventory service, and potentially currency conversion APIs. These dependencies introduce timing and state synchronization risks that threaten test stability.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: 22 Scenarios Require Comprehensive Test Data and Fixture Management
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: Total of 22 scenarios across 10 features require diverse test data: product catalogs with categories, user accounts with various permission levels, currency options, promotional campaigns, and search content. Managing this test data at scale requires a robust fixture strategy to ensure consistency, repeatability, and maintainability.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: 71 High-Confidence Findings (confidence >= 7) Require Mapped Test Coverage
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: DeFOSPAM analysis identified 71 findings with confidence >= 7, including 14 critical findings. These findings span ambiguous terms, missing business rules, and undefined requirements. Every finding with confidence >= 7 should have at least one corresponding BDD scenario to ensure validation during acceptance testing.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: CI/CD Pipeline Must Enforce Quality Gates at Multiple Stages
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: Validation strategy requires quality gates at four critical stages: (1) commit-level: smoke suite must pass 100%; (2) PR-level: regression suite must pass 95%+; (3) staging: release gate runs smoke in production-like environment; (4) post-release: production sanity checks. Without explicit gates, regressions will reach production.

findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.title: 13 of 20 Glossary Terms Remain at 'Proposed' Status - Domain Language Not Finalized
findings_sorted_by_severity_confidence.detail: Chris's language glossary shows only 7 of 20 terms at 'agreed' or 'verified' status. Remaining 13 terms are 'proposed' with undefined perspectives from business, developers, and testers. This indicate

business_goals.goal: Enable international users to view prices in their preferred currency, reducing friction in the purchase decision process for global customers
Establish secure access control to protect customer personal and payment information while enabling personalized user experiences
Enable logged-in users to save and manage product preferences for future consideration, creating a persistent engagement channel and reducing decision friction
Provide users with the ability to assemble and modify their purchase before checkout, reducing buyer anxiety and cart abandonment
Accelerate product discovery through intelligent search with autocomplete, reducing navigation friction and enabling users to find desired items rapidly
Provide organized product navigation structure enabling users to browse efficiently by product type, reducing cognitive load in product discovery
Drive immediate awareness and engagement with current promotions and featured products, leveraging homepage prominence to influence purchasing decisions
Enable rapid product evaluation on the homepage without requiring deep navigation, reducing friction in initial product consideration
Facilitate direct navigation from promotional content to detailed product information, enabling users to quickly access comprehensive product context
Enable users to independently resolve questions and learn platform usage, reducing support burden and improving user self-sufficiency

ubiquitous_language.definition: The monetary denomination selected by a user for displaying prices and conducting transactions within the e-commerce system
Product prices adjusted and displayed according to the user's selected currency and regional location
Users who have successfully authenticated to the system with valid credentials and maintain an active session
A curated collection of products saved by a user for future reference, comparison, or purchase consideration
A temporary virtual container where users accumulate selected products before initiating the checkout process
The process where users review cart items, provide payment and shipping information, and complete a purchase transaction
Dynamic search query predictions that appear as users type, based on product names, categories, and historical search patterns
Logical groupings of products organized by type (e.g., Computers, Electronics, Apparel, Digital Downloads, Books, Jewelry, Gift Cards) for navigation and discovery
Visual elements displayed on the homepage highlighting featured products, special offers, or marketing campaigns
Products selected for promotional visibility on the homepage due to sales performance, new releases, or marketing initiatives
